paper_title: Apple Pollination and Pollen Viability Study

methods.description: This method describes the process of hand-pollinating selected apple cultivars using different pollen sources to study the effects on flowering and fruit set.

steps.description: Select three commercial apple cultivars: 'Honeycrisp'/M.7, 'Fuji'/B.9, and 'Gala'/B.9.
Hand-pollinate selected flowers using one of three pollen sources: crabapple, 'Delicious', and 'Golden Delicious'.
steps.searchable_summary: hand-pollinate flowers with different pollen sources

steps.description: Collect temperature data from a weather station located less than one mile from experimental plots.
steps.searchable_summary: collect temperature data from weather station

steps.description: Design the experiment as a completely randomized design with two uniform adjacent trees for each cultivar.
steps.searchable_summary: design experiment with randomized tree selection

steps.description: Net trees to exclude bees and prevent cross-pollination.
Remove anthers at late pink stage to prevent self-pollination and hand-pollinate flowers using a small brush.

methods.description: This method tests the viability of pollen in the laboratory before pollination to ensure high germination rates.

steps.description: Place pollen on a medium of 1% agarose, 10% sucrose, and 10 ppm boric acid at room temperature for 24 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen medium for viability testing

steps.description: Visually observe the proportion of germination under a light microscope.

methods.description: This method evaluates pollen germination on the stigmatic surface and measures pollen tube growth using modified methods of Yoder et al. (2009).

steps.description: Collect five hand-pollinated flowers from each treatment at one, two, three, and four DAP.
Place flowers in a solution of 5% sodium sulfite, boil for 15 minutes, and refrigerate until examination.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare flowers in sodium sulfite solution

steps.description: Detach pistils, rinse with distilled water, and place in aniline blue stain solution on microscope slides.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pistils with aniline blue for microscopy

steps.description: Incubate pistils in the dark at room temperature for 24 hours and measure pollen tube length using epifluorescence microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen tube length with epifluorescence microscopy

steps.description: Rate pollen tube growth on the stigmatic surface and count pollen tubes penetrating the stigma.
steps.searchable_summary: rate and count pollen tube growth
